newsSince: No material new development since June 9. The LKCM Headwater $29.50/share take-private proposal (controlling-shareholder, ~79% owned) remains under independent special-committee review with no confirmed definitive agreement or close; the deal cap on upside is unchanged from the June 9 prior case.

sources.label: DSGR Confirms Receipt of Unsolicited LKCM Proposal (Business Wire)
LKCM aims to take DSGR private with $2B offer (MDM)

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent screen: the $29.50 LKCM take-private still pins DSGR in an arb band and caps upside, gutting the asymmetric latent-AI-node optionality. June 9 already applied that one-time correction; status is unchanged (no signed deal/close since), so no second move - hold at the corrected rank.
verdicts.bull: Specialty MRO / supply-chain platform could aggregate fragmented AI-infrastructure components if a deal falls through and optionality returns.
verdicts.neutral: Stock trades as deal arb near $29.50, decoupled from the latent-AI thesis; flat YTD reflects the cap.
verdicts.bear: Controlling-shareholder take-private at $29.50 caps upside ~8% and removes the asymmetric optionality this strict screen exists to capture.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Deal break/repricing that restores latent-AI optionality
